_description: Standardized JSON format for MCQ quizzes

questions.text: Mark the correct statements about geospatial data

options.text: Surface phenomena can be specified by a series of longitude, latitude and height.
Point phenomena are termed one-dimensional
Surface phenomena are termed three-dimensional.
Examples of area phenomena are cities, and streets.

questions.text: Which color scales are appropriate to communicate which information?

options.text: Map C is best suited to communicate the bettering or worsening of average income per county since last year
Map B is best suited to communicate population per square mile per state
Map A is best suited to communicate the bettering or worsening of average income per state since last year
Map A is best suited to communicate population per square mile per state

questions.text: Map projections are concerned with mapping the positions on the globe (sphere) to positions on the screen (flat surface).

options.text: Azimuthal projections do not preserve the direction from a central point.
In cone projections the degrees of longitude and latitude are usually orthogonal to each other.
Conformal projections do not retain local angles on each point of a map correctly.
Conformal projection does preserve shapes.

questions.text: Mark the correct statements regarding text visualisation.

options.text: ThemeRiver (stream graph) is a visualization of thematic changes in a document collection over time.
The semantic level of text representation deals with identifying and tagging each token's function inside the sentence.
Text visualization is useful to compare patterns in text documents.
Word clouds are visualizations of term frequencies as well as their context.

questions.text: Mark the correct statements

options.text: Choropleth maps communicate the geographical distribution of the represented statistical value better than cartograms.
Cartograms are based on the assumption that the mapped attribute is uniformly distributed in the geographical region.
Cartograms distort the geography according to the displayed statistical value.

questions.text: Concerning the aesthetics for dynamic visualization of graphs, mark the true sentences.

options.text: Concerning scalability, it only should be addressed for vertices and edges.
"Temporal aliases" do not impact significantly the quality of the layout.
"Reducing cognitive load" means reducing the amount of information the user has to keep in mind during the visualization of the graph.
Is preferable to preserve the mental map in the design of a dynamic network visualization method.

questions.text: A force-directed layout algorithm

options.text: models the graph after a physical system
is a method to reorder rows and columns of a matrix
is a graph-drawing method for node-link diagrams
is used to compute the colours of a heatmap
includes an energy maximization algorithm

questions.text: When designing a node-link graph visualization, particular attention has to be put on how to visualize the edges. Mark the correct sentences.

options.text: Polyline edges with multiple bends per edge can be used, but the number of bends should be minimized.
Curved approaches, such as Lombardi drawings, are usually preferred by the users even if they do not provide an advantage in any observed task.
In the directed case, when arrows are used to indicate the direction, they should not overlap with other edges or arrow heads.
Straight line node-link representations allow bends to reduce the number of crossings in the layout.

questions.text: A treemap

options.text: is a non-space-filling visualization method
is used to visualize hierarchies
can be squarified
can be nested
is used to visualize vegetation in a geographic area

questions.text:
Which of the following visual encodings for hierarchies satisfy the following description?
Features:
- Hierarchy visualized via parent-child relationship
- Layout techniques include "Layered" and "Radial" approaches
- No cycles
PROs:
- Intuitive
- Well-known
- Good at giving an overview of the data
CONs
- Large structures might not fit a single view
- Aspect ratio

options.text: Treemap
Node-Link (trees)
Indentation
Sunbursts
Icicle trees

questions.text: [!Q11](Resources/Q11_1.png)

options.text: shows different visual representations depending on the color-coding chosen by the user.
relies solely on geometric zooming.
shows different visual representations depending on the zoom level chosen by the user and the available space for the visualization.

allows users to choose between a large scope (i.e., zooming out and seeing an overview of the big picture) or a more detailed view (i.e., zooming in and seing all details about a limited range of the data).

questions.text: How does the use of Focus+Context techniques impact usability?

options.text: overview+detail and fisheye interfaces are beneficial for specific tasks
overview+detail and zooming interfaces impact users' performance negatively
Focus+Context techniques are easy to follow and can be used without prior knowledge
the effectiveness of Focus+Context techniques depends on the users' tasks and goals

questions.text: What levels of the Info-Vis Reference Model are influenced by dynamic querying (with real time feedback)?

options.text: Visual Mappings
Visual Structures
Data Transformations
Data Tables
Views
View Transformations
Raw Data

questions.text: Interaction modeling is employed to ...

options.text: ... validate effectiveness and user guidance of different interaction approaches.
... capture requirements the system can be developed and evaluated against.
... demonstrate interactions and tasks to users

questions.text: Coordinated Multiple View systems ...

options.text: use two or more distinct views to support the investigation of a single conceptual entity
combine different visualization techniques and apply them on different data representations
use color-coding for trend detection
can not be supported by brushing & linking techniques
always support semantic zooming
are often supported by brushing & linking techniques

options.text: random variables, different graphs
high level goals, correct measures

questions.text: Chernoff Faces

options.text: are suited to communicate complex data effectively
their features are likely to be perceived equally strong
are suspected to convey emotions
encode multiple data values with different graphical features

questions.text:
Glyph or icon based techniques
(Figure: example of a glyph: The Time Annotation Glyph represents temporal constraints of a medical treatment plan -- ESS: earliest starting shift, LSS: latest starting shift, EFS: earliest finishing shift, LFS: latest finishing shift, MaxDu: maximum duration, MinDu: minimum duration)

options.text: are composed of graphical primitives
cannot represent multiple data values at once
do not represent one meaning as a whole but encode multiple data values

questions.text: Parallel Coordinates

options.text: are better suited to visualize complex relations between two dimensions than scatterplots
re-ordering of the axes can reveal relations between two data dimensions
are usually arranged in a matrix to enable the visualization of multiple dimensions
represent each data dimension on one of multiple parallel axes

questions.text: Pixel-Based Techniques

options.text: represent a data item by (at least) one pixel
are suited for low-dimensional data sets only
value ranges of attributes are mapped to a color map

questions.text: "Semantic depth of field" (SDOF)

options.text: is good at visually communicating lots of different quantitative values
can be used to visually represent an additional data dimension in scatterplots
blurs data items based on their relevance

questions.text: Visual perception is...

options.text: a sequential process from eye to brain (bottom-up).
a combination of bottom-up and top-down processes.
a sequential process from brain to eye (top-down).

quantitative - interval
nominal

questions.text: Which of the following statements are correct?

options.text: a is not expressive, b is expressive
a and b are both expressive
a is equally expressive than b, but b is more effective

questions.text: Which of the following statements about mapping time to space are correct?

options.text: mapping of time to visual features
well suited for following trends and movements
direct comparison of parameters between different points in time is possible
not well suited for analytic and explorative tasks

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the visual mapping of time are correct

options.text: animation (time to time) mapping supports to follow trends and movements
animation (time to time) mapping is the simplest mapping
static (time to space) mapping is well suited for explorative tasks
static (time to space) mapping support direct comparison
animation (time to time) mapping is well suited for explorative tasks

questions.text: For data aggregation and data summary representations, which statements are correct?

options.text: Scatterplots of 2 dimensional data allow to confirm expected patterns.
Summary statistics of 2 dimensional data (mean, variance, correlation) are always different for different datasets.
Box-plots as summary representation show enough detail to distinguish different raw data characteristics.
Violin-plots as summary representation show more details about the structure as box-plots.

questions.text: According to the design study methodology (Sedlmair et al. 2012), when does a visualization design study offer a good solution for a given problem?

options.text: Area "X": all the required information is in the head of the user
Area "Y": either some of the required information is in the head of the user, or the task is not defined in a very clear way, or both.
Area "Z": the required information is available to the computer and the task is defined in a clear way.

questions.text: Formative evaluation

options.text: is used for problem analysis.
describes assessment methods after the implementation phase.
describes assessment methods during analysis and design phases.

questions.text: The central questions of problem analysis are:

options.text: What kind of data are the users working with?
What will the development method for the implementation phase be?
What are the tasks of the users?
How many software developers and designers are needed?
Who are the users of the system?

questions.text: What are the main benefits of user-centered design/human-centered aspects?

options.text: systems get more complex and the discomfort is increased.
product quality, aesthetics, and impact are reduced.
productivity of users is usually improved.
training and support costs are reduced.

questions.text: Selected goals of Visual Analytics are designing and creating methods to enable users to ...

options.text: detect the expected and discover the unexpected
render realistic images in an expressive way
synthesize information and derive insight from massive, dynamic, ambiguous, and often conflicting data

questions.text: Guidance is ...

options.text: a computer-assisted process that aims to harmonize heterogeneous information sources
a computer-assisted process that aims to ease data quality exploration
a computer-assisted process that aims to actively resolve a knowledge gap encountered by users during an interactive Visual Analytics session

maps, line chart, parallel coordinates, scatter plot, row charts, and networks
